text.title: Private-Beta / Design-Partner Cohort Recruitment Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Private-Beta / Design-Partner Cohort Recruitment application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every partner is vetted against the criteria list before NDA signature. Members who fail a criterion are declined with a named reason; no 'roster-fillers'.
rule.rationale: An unvetted member dilutes signal and inflates cohort cost.

rule.statement: Each partner signs the expectations contract (NDA + cadence + exit ramp). Verbal commitments are rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbal commitments evaporate; signed contracts give the cohort review-time leverage.

rule.statement: Cadence attendance is tracked per partner. Two consecutive misses triggers the exit-ramp review.
rule.rationale: Dead-weight partners inflate engagement metrics and crowd out active members.

rule.statement: Each partner contract names the value the operator delivers (early access, pricing lock, naming credit). Vague 'we'll keep you in the loop' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without named value exchange the cohort cannot self-renew.

rule.statement: The expectations contract carries an explicit exit ramp: how a partner leaves, what they keep, what they return.
rule.rationale: Cohorts without exit ramps accumulate ghost members and leak NDA scope.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
